methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pasqal is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Alain Aspect and Georges-Olivier Reymond, having raised €340M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a comp universe of 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private companies including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology does not apply here. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers total, but none satisfy the filtering criteria for reliable multiples work: zero companies match both stage and valuation tier simultaneously, and zero occupy adjacent development stages with sufficient overlap. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the technical and commercial heterogeneity across quantum platforms. Consequently, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be executed with the required minimum of three datapoints.

This analysis does not produce a triangulated valuation range. Resolution requires either (1) relaxation of stage-matching criteria with explicit risk disclosure, (2) introduction of alternative methods such as precedent transaction analysis within quantum infrastructure, or (3) direct management guidance on comparable funding rounds. The current dataset supports sector mapping and peer identification but not quantitative valuation synthesis. Further data collection on recent private financing rounds or public market multiples applicability is necessary before a defensible range can be established.
